content.claim: Hungary operates one of the most selective education systems in Europe, with school-based social sorting stronger than in Austria, Germany, Lithuania, and Netherlands according to OECD PISA 2015 indicators of between-school variation in learning outcomes.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Does not isolate the causal contribution of 6/8-year tracks alone to this selectivity
Attribution to multiple interacting causes (free school choice, admissions autonomy, tracking) rather than to the track structure in isolation
Analysis reflects 2015 data; more recent assessment data may show different patterns
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
